article_meta.title: Jurisprudence People Are Taking the Wrong Lesson From This Weekend's Dual Massacres By Ido Kirson and Austin Sarat Dec 1

reasoning: Generally focused on author prose and applied mostly appropriate labels, but included one clear false positive (a meta-critique not drawn from the article and mislabelled as Bias by Omission) and missed several prominent author-level bias instances (emotive framing in lede/headline, subjective labeling of Supreme Court, emotional appeals about fear).

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items were from the author’s prose, not quotes; no attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One item improperly cited a generalized summary not in the text and misapplied Bias by Omission.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear instances: emotive/loaded lede ('one of the more horrific weekends'), headline framing ('wrong lesson'), subjective 'pro-gun Supreme Court,' and explicit emotional appeals ('we are afraid').
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels were largely defensible for valid items (Spin, Opinion as Fact), but the Bias by Omission item was misapplied and not tied to a specific passage.
